help.whatIsCaseManagement: 案件管理让您可以使用可自定义的字段和工作流来追踪联系人、案件和事件。
help.gettingStarted: 从应用与您组织类型匹配的模板开始，或创建自定义实体类型。
help.templates: 模板为特定用例（如看守支持、街头急救或移民响应）提供预配置的案件类型、字段和建议角色。
help.entityTypes: 实体类型定义您的枢纽追踪的记录类型。每种类型都有自己的字段、状态和工作流。
help.contacts: 联系人是您的枢纽互动的人 — 来电者、被拘留者、患者、支持联系人。所有联系人数据都经过端到端加密。
help.encryption: 所有案件数据在到达服务器之前都会加密。只有授权的团队成员才能阅读案件内容。
help.search: 搜索通过比较加密令牌进行 — 服务器帮助筛选结果，但从未看到实际数据。
help.accessTiers: 数据组织为三个访问层级：摘要（团队可见）、详情（分配的志愿者和管理员可见）和受限（仅管理员可见）。
help.evidence: 证据文件经过加密并通过保管链追踪 — 每次查看、下载和分享都会被记录。
help.templatesHowto: 浏览下方可用模板。应用模板会自动创建案件类型和字段。您可以在应用后自定义所有内容。

crashReporting.privacyNote: Crash reports contain only technical information: error message, stack trace, app version, operating system, and device type. No personally identifiable information is collected.

gettingStarted.createShiftsDesc: Define shift schedules and assign volunteers

gettingStarted.inviteVolunteersDesc: Send invite links to your team members

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started

help.adminAuditDetail: Every action is logged in a tamper-evident chain. View who answered calls, created notes, and made changes. The hash chain ensures logs cannot be modified after the fact.
help.adminIntro: As an admin, you manage volunteers, shifts, call settings, and security features.
help.adminReportsDetail: Review incident reports filed by volunteers. View contact timelines showing all interactions with a caller. Caller identifiers are HMAC-hashed for privacy.
help.adminShiftsDetail: Set up recurring shift schedules with ring groups. Configure fallback groups for when no scheduled shift is active. Volunteers can only receive calls when clocked into their shift.
help.adminSpamDetail: Enable voice CAPTCHA to filter automated calls. Configure rate limiting per caller. Manage ban lists to block known bad actors. All settings can be adjusted in real-time.
help.adminTip1: Invite volunteers or reporters from the Volunteers page — choose their role when creating the invite
help.adminTip2: Set up shifts with time ranges and assigned volunteers for automatic call routing
help.adminTip3: Configure telephony, transcription, CAPTCHA, and rate limiting in Admin Settings
help.adminTip4: Monitor volunteer status and active calls from your dashboard
help.adminTip5: Review the Audit Log for a history of all system events
help.adminVolunteersDetail: Invite new volunteers with invite codes. Assign roles, manage permissions, and deactivate accounts as needed. Volunteer personal information is only visible to admins.
help.faqAdminA1: Go to Admin Panel > Invites and create a new invite code. Share the code securely with the volunteer. They'll use it during onboarding to register with your hub.
help.faqAdminA2: Go to Admin Panel > Ban List and add the caller's phone number. Banned callers will hear a message and be disconnected automatically.
help.faqAdminA3: Yes. The Audit Log in the Admin Panel shows all actions: calls answered, notes created, shifts started/ended, and more. The log is tamper-evident with hash chaining.
help.faqAdminQ1: How do I add a new volunteer?
help.faqAdminQ2: How do I ban a caller?
help.faqAdminQ3: Can I see all volunteer activity?
help.faqBreakA: When on break, incoming calls are not routed to you. Other on-shift volunteers will still receive them. Use the break toggle on your dashboard or press Ctrl+Shift+B.
help.faqCallsA1: Make sure you are clocked in for your shift and not on break. Check that your internet connection is stable (the relay connection indicator should be green).
help.faqCallsA2: If you don't answer, the call continues ringing for other on-shift volunteers. The first volunteer to pick up handles the call. Missed calls are logged in the dashboard.
help.faqCallsQ1: Why am I not receiving calls?
help.faqCallsQ2: What happens if I miss a call?
help.faqDeviceA: Go to Settings > Device Link and scan the QR code from your other device.
help.faqDeviceQ: How do I link another device?
help.faqEncryptA: Yes. Notes are encrypted end-to-end using your keypair before leaving your device. Only you and authorized admins can decrypt them. The server never sees plaintext.
help.faqExportA: Yes. Go to Notes and use the Export button. Notes are exported encrypted — you'll need your secret key to decrypt them.
help.faqGsA1: Ask your admin for an invite code. Enter the hub URL, create or import your identity, and set a PIN. You'll be ready to receive calls once you clock in for your shift.
help.faqGsA2: Your nsec is your secret key — like a password that proves your identity. It's generated on your device and never sent to the server. Back it up securely; if you lose it, you'll need a new identity.
help.faqGsA3: Yes. Use the Device Link feature in Settings to securely transfer your identity to another device using a QR code and encrypted key exchange.
help.faqInviteA: Go to Volunteers and click "Invite Volunteer". Choose the role (Volunteer, Admin, or Reporter), fill in their name and phone, and share the invite link. Each invite code is single-use and expires in 7 days.
help.faqKeyA: Your key is stored encrypted on this device, protected by your PIN. During onboarding you received a recovery key — use it to restore access on a new device. You can also link devices via QR code from Settings. Contact your admin if you've lost both your PIN and recovery key.
help.faqKeyQ: What is my secret key and where do I find it?
help.faqLoginA: Enter your 6-digit PIN on the login page to unlock your key store. If you have a passkey set up, you can use that instead. To set up a new device, use the "Link this device" option to scan a QR code from your primary device.
help.faqNotesA1: Yes. Notes are end-to-end encrypted with a unique key per note. Only you and your admins can decrypt them. The server never sees the plaintext content.
help.faqNotesA2: Notes are append-only for security. You can add additional notes but cannot modify existing ones. This ensures a reliable audit trail.
help.faqNotesQ2: Can I edit a note after saving?
help.faqProviderA: Go to Admin Settings > Telephony Provider. Select your provider, enter credentials, and test the connection before saving. Supported providers: Twilio, SignalWire, Vonage, Plivo, Asterisk.
help.faqProviderQ: How do I change the telephony provider?
help.faqPwaA: Yes. In your browser, look for 'Install' or 'Add to Home Screen' in the menu. The app will install as 'Hotline' for security — it doesn't reveal the organization name.
help.faqPwaQ: Can I install this as an app?
help.faqRingA: Check that: (1) you're on-shift (see your shift schedule), (2) you're not on break, (3) you're not already on a call, and (4) your browser/phone notifications are enabled.
help.faqRingQ: Why am I not receiving calls?
help.faqShiftsA: Create shifts in the Shifts page with a name, time range, and days of the week. Assign volunteers to each shift. When a shift is active, assigned volunteers receive incoming calls via parallel ringing.
help.faqShiftsQ: Where do I see my shifts?
help.faqSpamA: Three options: (1) Ban specific numbers in the Ban List, (2) Enable Voice CAPTCHA in Admin Settings to require callers to enter digits, (3) Enable Rate Limiting to block repeated calls from the same number.
help.faqSpamQ: How do I deal with spam calls?
help.faqVoicemailA: If no volunteer answers within the queue timeout, the caller is sent to voicemail (if enabled). Admins can configure the timeout and voicemail settings.
help.faqVoicemailQ: What happens if no one answers?
help.quickNavDesc: Press Ctrl+K to open the command palette for quick navigation and search.
help.reporterIntro: As a reporter, you can submit encrypted reports and communicate with administrators through a secure message thread.
help.reporterTip1: Click "New" to create a report with a title, category, and details
help.reporterTip2: Your report details are encrypted end-to-end — only you and admins can read them
help.reporterTip3: Reply to your own reports to provide updates or additional information
help.reporterTip4: Attach files to messages — they are encrypted before upload
help.secAuthDetail: Login uses cryptographic keypairs (Nostr). No passwords stored on servers.
help.secNotesDetail: Each note is encrypted with a unique key, wrapped for each authorized reader.
help.secReportsDetail: Reports are encrypted and only accessible to admins.
help.secSessionsDetail: Sessions are bound to your device via WebAuthn. Stolen tokens cannot be used elsewhere.
help.subtitle: How to use the app
help.title: Help
help.volCallsDetail: When a caller dials the hotline, all on-shift volunteers ring simultaneously. The first volunteer to pick up handles the call — all other rings are cancelled. Calls are routed based on your shift schedule and availability.
help.volEncryptionDetail: Your secret key (nsec) never leaves your device. Notes and messages are encrypted locally before being sent to the server. Even if the server is compromised, your data remains private.
help.volNotesDetail: Create notes during or after calls to document interactions. Notes support custom fields defined by your admin. All note content is end-to-end encrypted — only you and admins can read them.
help.volSafetyDetail: Lock the app when stepping away. Use PIN protection to prevent unauthorized access. If you're in a dangerous situation, use Emergency Wipe in Settings to permanently delete all app data from this device.
help.volShiftDetail: Clock in when your shift starts to begin receiving calls. Use break mode when you need a pause — you'll stop receiving calls but stay logged in. Clock out at the end of your shift.
help.volunteerIntro: As a volunteer, you answer incoming calls during your assigned shifts and take encrypted notes.
help.volunteerTip1: Your dashboard shows shift status, active calls, and incoming calls
help.volunteerTip2: When a call rings, click "Answer" — all on-shift volunteers ring simultaneously, first to answer gets the call
help.volunteerTip3: Take notes during calls — they're encrypted before leaving your device
help.volunteerTip4: Use 'Take a Break' when you need to step away — you won't receive calls
help.volunteerTip5: Report spam calls or ban numbers directly from the active call panel
help.cmsToggle: 启用案件管理以使用可自定义的字段和工作流来追踪联系人、案件和事件。
help.templateBrowser: 模板提供预配置的案件类型、字段和建议角色。您可以在应用后自定义所有内容。
help.entityTypes: 实体类型定义您的枢纽追踪的记录类型。每种类型都有自己的字段、状态和工作流。
help.schemaFields: 字段定义为每种案件类型收集的数据。设置访问级别以控制谁可以查看敏感信息。
help.statusPill: 点击更改案件状态。状态变更会自动记录在时间线中。
help.caseTimeline: 时间线显示此案件的所有活动：评论、状态变更、关联备注和证据上传。
help.evidenceTab: 证据文件使用完整性哈希存储以确保保管链。上传照片、文档或媒体文件。
help.contactLinking: 将联系人与角色（如被拘留者、律师或证人）关联到案件。联系人数据与案件数据分开加密。

bootstrap.noAdminMessage: No admin account configured yet. Complete the setup wizard to get started.
bootstrap.title: Create Admin Account
bootstrap.welcomeDescription: Let's create your admin account. Your cryptographic identity is generated in your browser — the server never sees your private key.

setup.channelReportsDesc: Encrypted reports submitted directly through the web app. Built-in, no provider needed.
setup.channelSignalDesc: Signal messaging for maximum caller privacy via a self-hosted bridge.
setup.channelSmsDesc: Text message conversations with callers via your hotline number.
setup.channelVoiceDesc: Traditional phone calls routed to on-shift volunteers via parallel ringing.
setup.channelWhatsappDesc: WhatsApp messaging for callers who prefer it over phone or SMS.
setup.channelsDescription: Select the channels your hotline will use. You can enable more later.
setup.complete: Setup complete! Welcome to your hotline.
setup.demoModeDescription: Creates sample volunteer accounts, shifts, and ban entries for testing and demos. Demo account credentials will be shown on the login page.
setup.hotlineNameHelp: This is shown to volunteers in the app. For security, the PWA uses a generic name.
setup.identityDescription: Choose a name and language for your crisis hotline.
setup.inviteDescription: Generate invite codes for your team. Each code is single-use and expires in 7 days.
setup.noProvidersNeeded: No external providers needed for your selected channels. You can proceed to the next step.
setup.noSettingsNeeded: No additional settings needed for your selected channels.
setup.pinRequired: Enter your PIN to continue setup.
setup.primaryLanguageHelp: The default language for the admin interface and IVR prompts.
setup.providersDescription: Set up the services that power your selected channels.
setup.queueTimeoutHelp: How long callers wait before being sent to voicemail.
setup.reportCategoriesHelp: Define categories for incoming reports (e.g. Harassment, Threats, Other).
setup.selectOneChannel: Please select at least one channel
setup.settingsDescription: Configure basic settings for your selected channels. You can fine-tune these later.
setup.signalDescription: Connect to a self-hosted Signal bridge for end-to-end encrypted messaging.
setup.summaryDescription: Review your hotline configuration before going live.
setup.title: Setup Wizard
setup.voicemailEnabledHelp: Allow callers to leave a voicemail if no one answers.
setup.whatsappTwilioNote: WhatsApp messages will be handled through your existing Twilio telephony provider. No additional configuration needed.

telephonyProvider.phoneNumberHelp: The phone number callers dial (E.164 format)

providerDescriptions.asterisk: Self-hosted PBX for maximum control and lowest per-minute cost. Requires server setup.
providerDescriptions.plivo: Cost-effective cloud telephony with a clean API. Good for budget-conscious deployments.
providerDescriptions.signalwire: Twilio-compatible API at lower cost. Drop-in replacement for most use cases.
providerDescriptions.twilio: Industry-standard cloud telephony. Easiest to set up with excellent documentation.
providerDescriptions.vonage: Global telephony provider with strong international coverage and competitive pricing.

telephonyProvider.title: Telephony Provider

telephonyProvider.webrtcConfigHelp: 为志愿者启用基于浏览器的通话。需要额外的凭据。
